data.title: U.K. Healthcare System to Offer Novo Nordisk's Wegovy Shot to 1.2 Million Heart Patients
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

The U.K.'s state-run healthcare system will offer Novo Nordisk's Wegovy weight-loss shot to over a million patients in England to help prevent heart attacks and strokes.

The decision comes after the country's independent health watchdog on Wednesday approved the drug for adults who have previously had a heart attack, stroke, or serious circulation problem in the legs, and who have a body mass index of at least 27.

The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) said that evidence from a clinical trial shows the injection reduces the risk of a heart attack, stroke, or cardiovascular death, with the benefit seen before significant weight loss occurred. This suggests the drug works directly on the heart and blood vessels, not just through weight loss, it said.

The trial showed that patients who took the treatment alongside existing medicines such as statins were 20% less likely to have a serious cardiovascular event than those who took a placebo.

"We know that people who have already had a heart attack or stroke are living with real fear that it could happen again," said Helen Knight, director of medicines evaluation at NICE.

"Today's decision gives thousands of people in that situation an extra layer of protection."

NICE said around 1.2 million people across England could be eligible for the once-weekly injection on top of existing medications and alongside a reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity to help protect against further heart attacks or strokes.

Wegovy is already available on the National Health Service for patients that meet certain criteria, while Ozempic, which shares the same active ingredient, is also available to treat patients with type 2 diabetes.

The drug is set to be available on the NHS to help prevent heart attack and stroke "within months," NHS England said in a statement.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Romania, Ukraine advance talks on EU-funded joint drone production
data.description:
Ukrainian drone manufacturers are meeting Romanian defence ministry and army officials in the capital Bucharest this week to discuss potential joint production under a new European Union rearmament funding mechanism, the ministry said in a statement.

Romania, an EU and NATO state, shares a 650-km (400-mile) land border with Ukraine and has had drones breach its airspace and fragments fall onto its territory repeatedly since Russia began attacking Kyiv's ports located across the Danube from Romania.

The EU has allotted Romania 16.6 billion euros ($19.2 billion) under its new rearmament initiative SAFE, which will begin later this year, and Defence Minister Radu Miruta said the country wanted to spend 200 million euros for joint drone production.

"We are currently discussing how to form a team to deal exclusively with this project, with the contract signing deadline being the end of May," Miruta said in a statement late on Tuesday after meeting Ukraine's Deputy Minister for Strategic Industries Sergiy Boyev.

Fifteen Ukrainian companies will continue discussing the project in Bucharest in the coming days, he added.

Earlier this month, Romanian and Ukrainian presidents signed a statement of intent to produce Ukrainian defence systems including drones in Romania.

($1 = 0.8630 euros)

data.title: Oil Falls on Hopes of Strait of Hormuz Reopening — Market Talk
data.description: Oil falls in Asia's afternoon trading session on hopes of the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway that handled around a fifth of global energy flows before the Middle East conflict. The U.A.E. is preparing to help the U.S. and other allies open the channel by force, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing Arab officials. The U.A.E. is lobbying for a U.N. Security Council resolution that would authorize such action, the WSJ reported, citing the officials. Front-month WTI crude oil futures fall 3.9% to $97.45 per barrel; front-month Brent crude oil futures drop 3.8% to $100.06 a barrel. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Europe's STOXXX 600 jumps 2% on Middle East de-escalation hopes
data.description:
Europe's STOXX 600 jumped more than 2% on Wednesday as U.S. President Donald Trump's comments that the month-long conflict in the Middle East could come to an imminent end sparked a global equities rally.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index was up 2.4% to 596.76 points by 0713 GMT, with all major sectors in the green except energy.

Most major regional bourses traded around 2% higher, with Germany's DAX leading with a 2.7% rise.

Oil price-sensitive travel stocks (.SXTP) jumped 4.2% and led sectors higher. Heavyweight banks (.SX7P) climbed 3.9%.

Investor sentiment globally perked up after Trump said the end of the war on Iran could be near, with Washington signalling potential for both direct talks with Tehran's leadership and a winding down of the conflict even without a deal.

"We'll be leaving very soon," Trump told reporters at the White House on Tuesday, saying the exit could take place "within two weeks, maybe two weeks, maybe three."

Brent crude futures slid more than 5% and were last down at $99.07 a barrel. The STOXX energy index (.SXEP) declined 0.7%

European shares have been under massive pressure since the U.S. and Israel began airstrikes on Iran on February 28 due to the continent's heavy reliance on oil imports through the largely blocked-off Strait of Hormuz.

The STOXX index had fallen more than 10% from its record high at one point earlier in March, and finished the month with losses that were its biggest since June 2022.

Shares of sportswear makers such as Adidas and Puma were up more than 3.6% each. U.S.- based rival had forecast a surprise drop in fourth-quarter sales.

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** The won strengthened more than 1% against the dollar and the benchmark bond yield dropped following the country's key global bond index inclusion.

** U.S. President Donald Trump and his Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the end of the war on Iran could be near, with Washington signalling potential for both direct talks with Tehran's leadership and a winding down of the conflict even without a deal.

** South Korea's exports beat forecasts in March, surging at their fastest pace in nearly four decades on an AI-driven chip boom, data showed.

** Among index heavyweights, chipmaker Samsung Electronics rose 13.40%, while peer SK Hynix gained 10.66%. Battery maker LG Energy Solution climbed 3.17%.

** Shares of Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were up 9.54% and 6.96%, respectively. Steelmaker POSCO Holdings added 4.06%, while drugmaker Samsung BioLogics rose 4.52%.

data.title: Wealth Taxes Can Be Skirted. 5 Smart Tips for the Rich. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Abby Schultz

There are plenty of ways the ultrarich can deflect the impact of a spate of state proposals to boost taxes on the wealthy, according to wealth advisors and tax attorneys.

Several states, and New York City, are considering taxing the ultrarich in some way as federal funding for public services such as healthcare, child care, and food assistance dwindles and as they seek to correct tax systems that put more burden, proportionally, on low and middle income taxpayers than the rich.

On March 30, the legislature in the state of Washington, which is considered one of the top 10 most regressive state and local tax systems in the country by the nonpartisan Institute on Taxation and Economic Policy, passed a 9.9% tax on household income of at least $1 million. To counter the blow, lawmakers also rolled back a hike in the top estate tax rate to 20% from 35%.

In California, a potential ballot initiative for a one-time 5% tax on the net worth of residents with $1 billion or more in assets has triggered an "exodus" of the ultrarich, according to these advisors. The initiative — which is still gathering signatures and isn't yet on the November ballot — would apply to billionaires living in California on Jan. 1, 2026.

That retrospective deadline pushed several billionaires to act quickly, including some high-profile residents. Travis Kalanick, the billionaire co-founder of Uber, told the tech podcast TBPN in early March that his primary residence was now in Texas. Sergey Brin and Larry Page are reducing their presence in the state where they co-founded Google by moving or closing several of their business entities, the New York Times reported in early January.

Depending on the type of tax enacted, however, the ultrarich can avoid paying higher taxes through tried-and-true estate planning techniques.

Jillyn Hess-Verdon, a partner at the law firm FBT Gibbons, has a list of items for her clients to consider proactively before any legislation aimed at the wealthy is approved where they live.

First up — revisit gifts made to an irrevocable trust, a vehicle that holds assets outside of an individual's estate for a beneficiary. If this kind of trust was established with a provision allowing assets to be swapped, it may make sense for someone to swap low-performing assets from the trust with those that have a better chance of growing in value over the next several decades, Hess-Verdon says.

Another option could be to set up a so-called dynasty trust in another state, Hess-Verdon says. These irrevocable trusts are created with provisions that can allow wealth to be passed along to future generations without incurring gift, estate, or generation-skipping transfer taxes — depending on how they are established.

About 20 states including South Dakota and Nevada allow dynasty trusts to exist forever, although several states put an end date on these trusts. "Jurisdictions that can handle dynasty trusts in perpetuity are going to be even more desired if there's a state billionaire tax," Hess-Verdon says.

The wealthy can also create another type of irrevocable trust called an Intentionally Defective Grantor Trust, or IDGT — a structure with its own tax identification number that keeps assets in an individual's estate for income-tax purposes, but allows them to grow tax-free and be passed on to beneficiaries without incurring estate taxes.

To fund an IDGT, a family could, for example, sell a $10 million asset to the trust by first gifting $100,000 (a portion of their lifetime gift tax exemption) as a down payment. The remaining $9.9 million would be sold to the trust, and the seller would receive a $9.9 million promissory note, Hess-Verdon says. The interest on that note can create an income stream for the seller, while the asset continues to grow in value in the trust and outside of their estate.

The U.S. Internal Revenue Service under President Donald Trump's administration has issued favorable rulings on IDGTs, making these vehicles a valuable tool. "Do it now under the current administration where the rules will be enforced and get those assets out of your estate, " she says.

Charitable trust planning can be another strategy. "Depending on the structure, there's either a gift to charity up front or a gift to charity at the end of the trust," that creates a charitable gift deduction for an individual and a "good chunk of money" for a charity," Chris Parker, a principal with law firm Baker Tilly's tax practice, says.

Time-tested tactics such as dynasty trusts and charitable planning offer the best options, Parker adds, cautioning the wealthy to avoid marketing ploys that draw them into the "new latest and greatest" tax structures, which are likely to be challenged by tax authorities.

Though many states want to raise taxes on the rich, others have either passed tax cuts, or are considering them, to make their states more attractive to wealthy residents and businesses.

As a result, the simplest advice wealth advisors and tax attorneys have for the ultrarich living in states that have taxes on high income or wealth — or are considering such taxes — is to move.

"The one benefit of being a state proposal is that the obvious solution is to get out of that state," Cara Koss, partner and head of private client services at the law firm Arnold & Porter says. "We certainly have many clients who have left high income-tax states."

Parker, who is based in California, has clients who have moved out of high tax states to places like Austin, Texas, Jackson Hole, Wyo., or Incline Village, Nev., on the shore of Lake Tahoe. Calls from clients in Washington state have jumped about 300%, he says.

On the East Coast, a millionaire tax enacted in Massachusetts in 2022, followed by legislation that boosted estate tax rates on wealthier residents in 2023, have prompted half of Paul Karger's Massachusetts clients to leave or consider leaving to low-tax states such as New Hampshire or Florida, he says.

"I tell clients don't let the tax tail wag the dog, so don't force your spouse to move to Puerto Rico or Florida or something if you don't actually want to be there," says Karger, co-founder of TwinFocus, a multifamily office for ultrawealthy families. "The truth is, a lot of the families I work with already have homes in other jurisdictions. It's not a big lift for them."

Arnold & Porter spends a lot of time advising clients on what it means to leave a state, and a primary residence, and what it means to establish residence elsewhere. "You have to be careful and pay attention to what the states test," Koss says. Some, such as California, have the resources to aggressively ensure residency is legally established, while others don't.

A federal tax on high-income levels or net worth could pose more complicated issues for the wealthy, however. Advisors don't see a reason to raise an alarm with their clients on this topic now, although several federal proposals have been introduced in Congress.

"Even if over the next several years, Congress and the presidency were to change parties, I don't know that the political appetite is there to take that bold of a step," Parker says.

On March 26, Democrats led by Sen. Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts reintroduced legislation for an "ultra-millionaire tax." The proposal aims to levy a 2% annual tax on the net worth of households and trusts over $50 million, in addition to a 1% annual surtax on the net worth of households and trusts above $1 billion.

Parker at Baker Tilly believes lawmakers interested in taxing wealth are more likely to make smaller changes to the tax code to "make it potentially more progressive than it is currently or shift the burden back up to the upper income earners and upper wealth holders on a long-term basis."

Federal wealth tax proposals are often floated, and "we'd be like 'Chicken Little' if we were constantly sending notifications about some proposal that may or may not get anywhere," Koss says.

The last time Arnold & Porter seriously reached out to clients about proposed federal legislation was when Congress considered language in the Build Back Better Act of 2021 that would have changed the status of grantor trusts to include their assets in an owner's taxable estate. "We were in a frenzy about that one," she says.

But provisions in proposed legislation, such as moving up the effective date of a new law as could happen in California, shows lawmakers are getting smarter about designing tax law, Koss says. "By the time we hear about it, it's too late to plan around it."

Write to Abby Schultz at abby.schultz@barrons.com

data.title: OMCs stare at significant losses on domestic LPG sales; govt likely to take a fiscal hit on compensating OMCs
data.description:
The Finance Ministry is likely to see a significant fiscal hit on India’s oil maths going haywire due to the war in West Asia.

As things stand today, the Oil Marketing Companies are incurring under-recoveries of ₹380/domestic LPG cylinder, the Ministry of Petroleum said. According to the Ministry, this may translate into cumulative losses of approximately ₹40,484 cr by the end of May this fiscal.

Last fiscal year, the finance ministry compensated OMCs to the tune of ₹30,000 cr out of total losses of ₹60,000 cr incurred by the OMCs on the sale of subsidised domestic LPG, the Petroleum Ministry said.

OMCs have increased the commercial LPG cylinder price for the second time since the war began but refrained from doing so for domestic LPG.

The Petroleum Ministry said, "The April 1 price increase in Commercial cylinder price is due to a 44% surge in the Saudi Contract Price: from $542/MT in March to $780/MT for April, as 20-30% of global LPG supplies are stuck in the Strait of Hormuz."

On March 27, the finance ministry cut the excise on the sale of petrol and diesel by ₹10/litre each. This is likely to cause a net revenue loss of ₹5500 cr to the exchequer every fortnight, CBIC had said, after factoring in the revenue gain of ₹1500 cr every fortnight on the levy of export duty on ATF and diesel.

The export duties will be reviewed every 14 days.

data.title: UK gilt yields and rate bets fall on hopes for end to Iran war
data.description:
Yields on British government bonds fell sharply on Wednesday and investors scaled back their expectations for interest rate hikes by the Bank of England after U.S. President Donald Trump signalled the Iran war could come to an end soon.

Interest rate futures were fully pricing in one 25 basis-point increase in the BoE's Bank Rate by the end of 2026 and the possibility of a second compared with two or three hikes on Tuesday.

data.title: Russian Manufacturing Downturn Deepens In March
data.description:
MOSCOW (dpa-AFX) - Russia's factory activity deteriorated further in March amid faster decreases in output and new orders, survey results from S&P Global showed on Wednesday.

The manufacturing purchasing managers' index, or PMI, dropped to 48.3 in March from 49.5 in February. Any reading below 50 indicates contraction.

New orders fell at the fastest pace in five months due to competition and reduced consumer purchasing power, and export demand was weaker in March.

Consequently, output declined at the steepest rate in three months, and they also scaled back the purchasing activity the most in four years. Manufacturing employment fell for the fourth straight month, linked to redundancies following lower output levels and moves to piecework pay.

On the price front, input costs rose at the second-fastest pace in just over a year due to higher fuel and supplier costs. However, selling price inflation eased amid competition and efforts to drive new sales.

Looking ahead, customer purchasing power concerns dipped the business confidence to the weakest in almost four years.

data.title: Spanish manufacturing shrinks in March at fastest rate since April 2025, PMI shows
data.description:
Spain's manufacturing sector contracted in March at its fastest pace since April 2025 as the Middle East war hit output and demand, a business survey showed on Wednesday.

The S&P Spain Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) fell to 48.7 in March from 50.0 in February. The 50 mark separates growth from contraction.

"Spain's manufacturing economy returned to contraction territory during March as the Middle East war put pay to any hopes in February of a short-term improvement in performance," said Paul Smith, economics director at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

Output fell for a second successive month in March, with the decline the fastest since December 2023. New orders contracted steeply and export demand fell even faster, with both measures posting their weakest readings since April 2025.

Confidence about production over the coming 12 months dropped to its lowest since October 2023. Employment fell for a seventh straight month and at the sharpest pace since October 2023, as firms mainly chose not to replace departing staff.

Supply chains also came under renewed strain. Delivery times lengthened by the most since September 2022, while input price inflation accelerated to its highest since October 2022 and factory gate inflation hit its strongest level since February 2023.

The outlook remains tied to how the Middle East conflict develops, with manufacturers citing fears of a prolonged global slowdown and higher energy-driven inflation.

data.title: Yuanda China's 2025 Operating Revenue Surged 27% to RMB 2.8 Billion, with a Net Profit of RMB 0.35 Billion, turning losses into gains
data.description:
HONG KONG, Apr 1, 2026 - (ACN Newswire) - On 31 March 2026, Yuanda China Holdings Limited (Stock Code: 02789.HK, "Yuanda China"), a global leader in the curtain wall industry, announced its audited annual consolidated results for the year ended 31 December 2025 (the "Reporting Period").

In 2025, competition in the building curtain wall industry continued to intensify, placing higher demands on enterprises' technical capabilities, project management, and financial operational capability. In light of the market conditions in 2025, the Group adhered to the business principle of "prudent operation, quality improvement and efficiency enhancement, and risk control", and fully leveraged its professional strengths. During the year, the Group continued to deepen refined management, focusing on lean control throughout the entire project lifecycle to effectively improve engineering quality and delivery efficiency. At the same time, the Group strengthened supply chain coordination and strictly controlled procurement costs to further consolidate cost competitiveness. In terms of market expansion, the Group focused on quality overseas clients and high-quality projects, proactively avoiding high-risk orders to ensure healthy and stable cash flow. Furthermore, the Group deepened technological innovation, transformed its production model, obtained 7 new utility model patents, and has built a product system with core technological competitive advantages. In 2025, the Group achieved steady and robust growth by leveraging sound business strategies and exceptional project execution capabilities.

Benefiting from the tangible results of continuously improving operational efficiency and the successful delivery of core projects both domestically and internationally, the Group's operating revenue in 2025 increased significantly by 27.2% on a year-on-year basis to approximately RMB 2,814.6 million (Unit: RMB, the same below), among which, the domestic revenue amounted to approximately RMB 1,271.6 million, with an increase of 25.1% compared with 2024, contributing approximately 45.2% of the Group's total revenue; the overseas revenue amounted to approximately RMB 1,543.0 million, with an increase of 29.0% compared with 2024, contributing approximately 54.8% of the Group's total revenue. Meanwhile, driven by its international strategy, the Group achieved notable growth in overseas markets, particularly in Australia and the Middle East. During the Reporting Period, the total value of new projects secured by the Group amounted to approximately RMB 5,168.3 million, with an increase of approximately 55.4% compared with 2024. As at 31 December 2025, the outstanding contract value of the Group amounted to approximately RMB 13,201.3 million, which provides strong support for the Group's development over the next two to three years.

In addition, the Group focused on quality customers, enhancing both coverage and depth, while increasing the proportion of newly secured projects with higher gross profit margins. Benefiting from these initiatives, the Group's gross profit margin steadily improved and profitability continued to strengthen. In 2025, the Group's gross profit margin was approximately 26.1%, with an increase of approximately 6.6 percentage points compared with 2024. Furthermore, the Group continuously strengthened the collection management of trade receivables and contract assets. During the year, the receivables turnover days decreased by 41.0% on a year-on-year basis to approximately 184 days, while the turnover days of trade and bills payables decreased by 50.6% on a year-on-year basis to approximately 214 days, reflecting a comprehensive improvement in capital turnover efficiency. During the Reporting Period, the Group's adjusted gross profit margin increased significantly by 18.8 percentage points to approximately 30.3%, demonstrating strong profit quality. For the Reporting Period, the profit for the year attributable to equity shareholders of the Company was approximately RMB 352.5 million, successfully turning losses into gains compared with a loss of RMB 354.0 million in 2024.

Looking ahead to 2026, the building curtain wall industry still faces multiple pressures such as slow demand recovery, intensified competition and cost fluctuations. However, the industry will also usher in a strategic opportunity period for technological upgrading and green transformation, where innovative products such as prefabricated curtain walls and building integrated photovoltaics are expected to accelerate their penetration, and digital transformation will remain the main path for industry development. The Group will always adhere to a prudent and stable operating philosophy to integrate risk control throughout the business process. Leveraging the core strengths, the Group will closely monitor market changes, rationally plan the business layout, and ensure the smooth and orderly development of the business. In the future, the Group will balance efficiency improvement and risk control, consolidate the existing core market share through refined management, focus on core regional markets and prioritize projects with manageable risks. The Group will further strengthen technological research and development, enhance system integration capabilities, and create more benchmarking curtain wall projects; deepen internal management reforms, optimize resource allocation, and continuously improve operational efficiency and profitability. At the same time, the Group will strictly adhere to the risk bottom line to ensure capital security and promote the Group's high-quality and sustainable development, thereby creating greater value for shareholders and delivering more high-quality projects.

Copyright 2026 ACN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: ZAWYA: $287mln Algerian-Qatari-German hospital project nears completion
data.description:
Majda Muhsen

Algerian Qatari German Hospital, a public-private partnership (PPP) project undertaken by Qatar’s Apex Health with Algeria’s National Investment Fund (FNI) is nearing completion, according to a report by local news portal Echoroukonline.

The $287 million hospital, which is being constructed in the city of Sidi Abdellah, is expected to be delivered in 2027, the news portal reported last week.

The 300-bed hospital spans an area of around 10 hectares, including approximately 6 hectares of built-up space, and is being built by SPA Algerian Qatari Healthcare Services Company, a statement issued by Algerian Investment Promotion Agency (AAPI) said.

The development is expected to create around 954 direct jobs, in addition to indirect employment opportunities across the healthcare and construction sectors.

Apex Health is the healthcare subsidiary of Estithmar Holding.

data.title: Japan futures climb as tight synthetic supply prompts shift to natural rubber
data.description:
By Ruth Chai

  • Japanese rubber futures rose on Wednesday, recouping from losses in the previous session, as high butadiene rubber prices and tight synthetic rubber supply prompted manufacturers to substitute towards natural rubber.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for September delivery , closed trade 1.4 yen, or 0.37%, higher at 382 yen ($2.41) per kg.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for May delivery fell 25 yuan, or 0.15%, to 16,415 yuan ($2,388.02) per metric ton.

  • The most-active May butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) slipped by 760 yuan, or 4.33%, to 16,785 yuan per ton.

  • As butadiene rubber prices continue to stay above natural rubber prices, the profit margins of synthetic rubber producers have been drastically squeezed, resulting in losses, Guoyuan Futures Research said in a note on WeChat.

  • The capacity utilisation rates of China's synthetic rubber industry had dropped from the average rate of around 80% to around 50% end-March, Guoyuan Futures Research said.

  • The decline in industry operating rates cannot solely be attributed to short-term factory maintenance shutdowns. It is also due to systemic production cuts resulting from an imbalance of profit distribution across the supply chain, said Zhang Xiao, an analyst from Guoyuan Futures Research.

  • Tighter supply of butadiene rubber would push natural rubber prices up as product manufacturers substitute synthetic rubber for natural rubber.

  • Oil tumbled more than 3% on Wednesday, reversing earlier gains as persistent Middle East volatility unnerved markets even amid reports the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran could be winding down.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for May delivery last traded at 199.4 U.S. cents per kg, up 1.6%, of 0706 GMT.

($1 = 158.4200 yen)

data.title: Baidu's Apollo Go Robotaxis Stall in China's Wuhan
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

A number of Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxis froze in the middle of traffic in a major Chinese city, forcing passengers to abandon the vehicles, and raising questions over the safety of self-driving cars.

The Wuhan police department said it received multiple reports on Tuesday evening that Apollo Go cars had stalled on the streets of the city, and were unable to move.

The police said preliminary findings indicated that the incidents were caused by a system failure. All passengers safely exited the vehicles and there were no injuries, the police added.

Baidu didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from The Wall Street Journal.

Apollo Go has been expanding its robotaxi fleet size both domestically and overseas. Wuhan is one of Apollo Go's major robotaxi hubs.

Apollo Go completed 3.4 million driverless rides in the fourth quarter last year, with total rides more than tripling from the prior-year period.

This year is critical as companies start to reap benefits with the scaling up of robotaxi fleets, HSBC Global Research analysts said in a note.

One major uncertainty weighing on the robotaxi industry is safety. This most recent incident is a rare occurrence for robotaxi companies in China after the widespread rollout of such vehicles in major cities across the country.

"While a great deal of progress has been made in China to approach the commercial inflection, efforts to increase consumer acceptance still have a long way to go," HSBC added.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com
